label: 342_debt relief_greece_greeces_greek
description: Greece's third economic programme has seen some success with a primary fiscal surplus and resumed growth. Recent deals with the Eurogroup avoid actual debt relief but aim for sustainability, highlighting the challenges in forecasting future debt scenarios.

earliest_articles.title: Greek Debt Dynamics: Daunting but Feasible
earliest_articles.excerpt: Recent market volatility seems to reflect severe doubts that the Greek rescue package can work and, by extension, that serious contagion to other larger European debtors can be avoided. Yet a close look at the numbers in the IMF program for Greece suggests that although the fiscal adjustment needed to secure debt…

earliest_articles.title: Greece, the unbearable heaviness of debt
earliest_articles.excerpt: Many analysts long ago predicted that Greece would have to default on part of its debt or, as they say in the markets, investors would have to take a “haircut.”1 It looks like they are getting closer to being right. On 9 May 2011, Standard & Poors downgraded the Greek debt by reducing its ratings from BB- to B, for…

newest_articles.title: Greece: A postponed tranche, relief for pensioners, and likely budget endorsement
newest_articles.excerpt: The tangled web of Greek finances is a long-running saga whose current chapter is complicated. However, glimmers of relief are beginning to appear. Bright spots include the likely endorsement of the Greek budget by the Eurogroup on December 3. Yet, all is not sanguine and EU officials should avoid wearing rose colored…

newest_articles.title: Is the latest Greek debt deal sustainable or another kick of the can?
newest_articles.excerpt: The Eurogroup recently granted Greece relief through an extension of loans that were part of the second program of the European Financial Stability Facility and extended Greece’s most pressing loan maturities by 10 years (see Figure 1). The country was also given a 15 billion euro tranche from the 86 billion euro…

central_articles.title: Putting the Greek debt problem to rest
central_articles.excerpt: Unlike its two predecessors, Greece’s third economic programme has been relatively successful. Since 2015, the country has been enacting most of the conditions attached to its loans. It primary fiscal balance is back in surplus, and growth has resumed. Both the government and its official creditors are hoping that…

central_articles.title: The Eurogroup on Greece: Debt Relief with a Fiscal Straitjacket
central_articles.excerpt: Greece’s latest deal with the Eurogroup—the group of eurozone finance ministers—is bigger news than you might think from the press reactions so far. As expected, Greece got its next disbursement of funds to avoid default, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) gave a symbolic stamp of approval, no actual debt relief…
